questions.q: What constitutional office did H.D. Deve Gowda hold at the Union level from 1996 to 1997?
questions.explanation: H.D. Deve Gowda served as the Prime Minister of India from 1996 to 1997.

questions.q: Deve Gowda became Prime Minister at the head of which coalition?
questions.explanation: He headed the United Front coalition government formed after the 1996 general election.

questions.q: Before becoming Prime Minister, Deve Gowda served as the Chief Minister of which state?
questions.explanation: He had earlier served as Chief Minister of Karnataka before becoming Prime Minister.

questions.q: In which year did Deve Gowda become Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: Deve Gowda became Prime Minister in 1996 following that year's general election.

questions.q: Deve Gowda's subtitle in this deck, 'Son of the Soil,' reflects his association with which kind of issues?
questions.explanation: He is widely associated with rural and agrarian concerns, reflected in the 'Son of the Soil' description.

questions.q: Deve Gowda's term as Prime Minister came after which Prime Minister's full term ended in 1996?
questions.explanation: Deve Gowda became Prime Minister in 1996, succeeding P.V. Narasimha Rao who had served from 1991 to 1996.

questions.q: How long did Deve Gowda's tenure as Prime Minister last?
questions.explanation: His tenure lasted roughly a year, from 1996 to 1997.

questions.q: Deve Gowda's career before becoming Prime Minister was primarily rooted in the politics of which region of India?
questions.explanation: He rose through politics in Karnataka in southern India before reaching the Union level.

questions.q: Which of the following best describes the government Deve Gowda led as Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: He led the United Front, a multi-party coalition government.

questions.q: The office Deve Gowda held in 1996-1997 makes him the head of which branch of the Union?
questions.explanation: As Prime Minister he was the head of the Union government, which holds the real executive authority.

questions.q: Deve Gowda was sworn in as Prime Minister in which month of 1996?
questions.explanation: He was sworn in as Prime Minister on 1 June 1996.

questions.q: Deve Gowda became Prime Minister after the general election to which Lok Sabha?
questions.explanation: He became Prime Minister following the 1996 election that constituted the 11th Lok Sabha.

questions.q: When Deve Gowda was sworn in as Prime Minister in June 1996, he was a sitting member of which legislature?
options: He held no legislative seat at all
questions.explanation: At the time he took office as Prime Minister in 1996 he was a member of the Karnataka Legislative Assembly; he later entered the Rajya Sabha within the constitutionally required six months.

questions.q: Which President of India administered the oath of office to Deve Gowda as Prime Minister in 1996?
questions.explanation: Shankar Dayal Sharma was the President of India in 1996 when Deve Gowda was sworn in.

questions.q: Who succeeded Deve Gowda as Prime Minister in 1997 within the same coalition framework?
questions.explanation: I.K. Gujral succeeded Deve Gowda as Prime Minister in April 1997.

questions.q: Deve Gowda's term as Chief Minister of Karnataka began in which year?
questions.explanation: He became Chief Minister of Karnataka in 1994, before moving to national politics in 1996.

questions.q: Deve Gowda is associated with the founding leadership of which regional party in Karnataka?
questions.explanation: He is the founding leader of the Janata Dal (Secular), a party with a strong base in Karnataka.

questions.q: The United Front government led by Deve Gowda was supported from outside by which major national party?
questions.explanation: The Indian National Congress provided outside support to the United Front government.

questions.q: Deve Gowda's rise to Prime Minister in 1996 is often cited as an example of which broader feature of that era of Indian politics?
options: Direct election of the Prime Minister
questions.explanation: His premiership exemplifies the era of coalition governments that began in the late 1980s.

questions.q: Which year marked the end of Deve Gowda's tenure as Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: His tenure as Prime Minister ended in 1997 when he was succeeded by I.K. Gujral.

questions.q: In which language-speaking region did Deve Gowda build his primary political base?
questions.explanation: His political base lay in the Kannada-speaking state of Karnataka.

questions.q: Counting from independence, the office Deve Gowda held in 1996 had previously been held by how many Prime Ministers from southern India who served full terms?
questions.explanation: P.V. Narasimha Rao was the first Prime Minister from South India to serve a full term, preceding Deve Gowda.

questions.q: On which date in 1996 did Deve Gowda take the oath as Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: Deve Gowda was sworn in as Prime Minister on 1 June 1996.

questions.q: Deve Gowda first served as Chief Minister of Karnataka from 1994 until he resigned in which year to take up national office?
questions.explanation: He stepped down as Karnataka Chief Minister in 1996 to become Prime Minister.

questions.q: Which constitutional provision governs the appointment of the Prime Minister, the office Deve Gowda held?
questions.explanation: Article 75 provides that the Prime Minister is appointed by the President, the office Deve Gowda assumed in 1996.

questions.q: Deve Gowda's United Front government drew together how many parties broadly described as a 'third force' outside the two largest national parties?
options: A coalition of several regional and left parties
A bloc of only Union Territory representatives
questions.explanation: The United Front was a coalition of several regional and left-leaning parties forming a non-Congress, non-BJP alignment.

questions.q: In the Karnataka legislative tier, the Vidhan Sabha that Deve Gowda led as Chief Minister is which kind of body?
questions.explanation: The Vidhan Sabha he headed as Chief Minister is the directly elected Legislative Assembly of Karnataka.

questions.q: Deve Gowda represented his region in which capacity in Parliament during much of his later career after his premiership?
options: As Speaker of the Lok Sabha
As an elected member of the Lok Sabha
questions.explanation: He continued his parliamentary career as an elected member of the Lok Sabha from Karnataka after his term as Prime Minister.

questions.q: Deve Gowda's premiership fell between the terms of which two full-term Prime Ministers spanning 1991 to 2004?
options: P.V. Narasimha Rao and Atal Bihari Vajpayee
Rajiv Gandhi and Atal Bihari Vajpayee
V.P. Singh and P.V. Narasimha Rao
questions.explanation: His 1996-1997 tenure came after P.V. Narasimha Rao's full term and before Atal Bihari Vajpayee's 1999-2004 full term.

questions.q: As Prime Minister, Deve Gowda headed which constitutional body that aids and advises the President in executive functions?
questions.explanation: As Prime Minister he headed the Council of Ministers, which aids and advises the President under the Constitution.
